modules.title: IoT definition, characteristics and architecture
topics: Physical and logical design of IoT
Review of basic microcontrollers and interfacing

modules.title: Sensors and actuators
topics: Basic components and challenges of a sensor node
Sensor classes: analog, digital, scalar, vector sensors
Actuator types: hydraulic, pneumatic, electrical, thermal/magnetic, mechanical, soft actuators

topics: Wireless sensor networks and their applications

modules.title: IoT platforms, analytics and security
topics: IoT platforms (Arduino, Raspberry Pi, other platforms)

modules.title: Understanding Blockchain with Cryptocurrency

topics: Execution of smart contracts in enterprise settings

modules.title: Introduction to modern processors
topics: General purpose cache‑based architecture performance metrics and benchmarks
Vector processors – design principles, maximum performance estimates, programming for vector architecture
Basic optimizations for serial codes – scalar profiling, common‑sense optimizations, simple measures and their impacts, role of compilers, C++ optimizations

modules.title: Data access optimizations
topics: Parallel computers – shared memory computers, distributed memory computers, hybrid systems, network computers

modules.title: Basics of parallel computing
topics: Parallel scalability – laws, metrics, factors, efficiency, load imbalance
Shared‑memory parallel programming with OpenMP – parallel execution, data scoping, work sharing using loops, synchronization, reductions, loop scheduling, tasking

modules.title: Efficient OpenMP programming
topics: Improving the impact of OpenMP work‑sharing constructs

modules.title: Distributed memory parallel programming with MPI
topics: Impact of synchronizations, serializations and contentions

modules.title: Introduction to RUP

reference_books: CLARK, T., OSTERWALDER, A., PIGNEUR, Y. – Business model generation: a handbook for visionaries, game changers, and challengers. Wiley Publications.
BESSANT, J R., TIDD, J. – Managing innovation: integrating technological, market and organizational change. Wiley Publications.
subjects.extra: The aim of the course is to motivate students to innovate in business. Students are introduced to basic terminology, typology of innovations, historical context, innovation management issues, impact of innovation, innovative processes, applicable methods and innovation management techniques.
